etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) ('walk; movement') + phonetic 从 (sound) -> follow
etymology.interpretation: 從(從) (cóng, 'to follow') combines the idea of 'walking' or 'movement' from 辵(辵) (chuò, 'walk; movement') with the sound symbolized by 从 (cóng, 'follow'), capturing the concept of moving along a path under guidance or direction.
etymology.simplification: 从 is a variant form directly used as the simplified form, adopting the earlier, less complex writing as the standard.

examples.chinese: 你需要从这里开始(你需要從這裡開始)。
examples.english: You need to start from here.

examples.chinese: 她从小就喜欢画画(她從小就喜歡畫畫)。
examples.english: She has liked drawing since she was a child.

examples.chinese: 所有东西都从他的袋子里掉出来了(所有東西都從他的袋子裡掉出來了)。
examples.pinyin: Suǒyǒu dōngxī dōu cóng tā de dàizi lǐ diào chūláile.
examples.english: Everything fell out of his bag.
